title: Request for Unified Communications & Microsoft Teams Phone System
summary: REQUEST FOR QUOTATION Unified Communications & Microsoft Teams Phone System Clear Creek County, Colorado Issuing Department: Clear Creek County IT Department RFQ Number: RFQ #IT-2026-002 Issue Date: June 24, 2026 Submission Deadline: July 8, 2026 at 5:00 PM MDT Submission Method: Email only -- see Section 5 for instructions Contact / Submit To: For security reasons, you must enable JavaScript to view this E-mail address. Award Basis: Best overall value -- price is one factor among several 1. Purpose and Overview Clear Creek County IT Department is soliciting competitive quotations from qualified Unified Communications as a Service (UCaaS) providers for a Microsoft Teams-integrated phone system serving Georgetown, Colorado. This is an open public request; any qualified vendor may respond. The County operates an active Microsoft 365 tenant and seeks a provider to deliver Teams Operator Connect or Direct Routing-based telephony, cloud fax, call recording, SMS/text messaging, and associated hardware and implementation services. This RFQ is not a contract or a commitment to purchase. The County reserves the right to accept or reject any or all quotations, to waive any informalities, and to award to the vendor deemed most advantageous to the County. IMPORTANT: Award Notice -- Value Over Price Alone Vendors will NOT be selected solely on the basis of price. The County will evaluate proposals holistically, considering technical capability, service quality, implementation support, contract flexibility, references, and long-term value. Submitting the lowest price does not guarantee award. 2. Current Environment & Context Vendors should understand the County's existing environment when preparing proposals: - Primary location: Georgetown, CO 80444 - Approximately 233 users / DID lines requiring telephony services - Active Microsoft 365 tenant; Teams is the primary collaboration platform - Approximately 50 mobile users requiring Teams mobile integration - Fax requirements: approximately 10 cloud fax lines + 5 analog / MasterFax-style lines - Call recording required for approximately 3 users (compliance-grade) - SMS/text messaging required for approximately 20 Teams users - 2 conference / meeting room endpoints - Number porting required for approximately 233 US/CAN DIDs 3. Scope of Services Required Vendors must provide a complete solution covering all categories below. Partial proposals will not be considered unless the vendor clearly identifies which items involve third-party subcontracting and names the subcontractor. 3A. Teams Voice -- Operator Connect or Direct Routing - MS Teams Operator Connect (preferred) or Direct Routing -- US/CAN unlimited calling plan - Approximately 230 user seats, unlimited US/CAN inbound and outbound - 2 Resource Account Calling Plans (Auto Attendant / Call Queue) - 2 CT Cloud Voice / Basic SIP lines for non-Teams analog extensions - 3 active US DIDs (additional numbers beyond ported set) 3B. DID / Number Management & E911 - Porting of approximately 233 existing US/CAN telephone numbers - E911 surcharge and dynamic E911 compliance for all active lines - DID assignment professional services for MS Teams tenant configuration 3C. Cloud Fax - 10 cloud fax lines -- inbound and outbound, fax-to-email / email-to-fax - 5 analog / MasterFax-style unlimited lines with ATA adapters 3D. Call Recording - Compliance-grade call recording for approximately 3 users - Must include: unlimited audio retention, playback, tagging, AI transcription/summarization, and advanced admin licenses - Must be compatible with Microsoft Teams Compliance Recording API or equivalent 3E. SMS / Text Messaging for Teams - SMS/MMS standard license for approximately 20 Microsoft Teams users - Must support individual and shared inboxes, group messaging, and Teams-native notifications - 10DLC SMS campaign registration required 3F. Mobile Integration - Mobile background data or Teams mobile dialer solution for approximately 50 users 3G. summary: The City of Wheat Ridge is soliciting proposals from qualified individuals, institutes, firms, or organizations to complete a Nexus Study for a Housing Impact (Linkage) Fee. The goal is to assess the demand for affordable housing generated by both residential and commercial development and to explore a legally defensible fee structure. The study should evaluate the potential impact on future development activity in Wheat Ridge and whether a linkage fee would be economically viable. This study will directly support the implementation of the City's Affordable Housing Strategy and Action Plan (AHS) by exploring a linkage fee as a secondary revenue source for the City’s Housing Fund. City Council provided this direction based on the results of a consultant-led Inclusionary Housing Zoning (IHZ) Feasibility Analysis, which indicated that an IHZ program may not be the most appropriate tool for Wheat Ridge at this time due to market constraints and development patterns.

title: 2026 Sewer Rehabilitation Project
summary: Southgate Sanitation District 2026 Sewer Rehabilitation: Sealed Bids for construction of the 2026 Sewer Rehabilitation will be received by the SOUTHGATE SANITATION DISTRICT (the "Owner") at the office of the District, 3722 East Orchard Road, Centennial, Colorado 80121, and publicly opened and read aloud at 1:00 pm on June 30, 2026. The 2026 Sewer Rehabilitation work generally consists of the following: CIPP (cured-in-place pipe) lining of approximately 1,164-linear feet of 12-inch sewer using UV-cured resin material. CIPP (cured-in-place pipe) lining of approximately 6,244-linear feet of 8-inch sewer using UV-cured resin material OR steam cured material. Both options will be considered for 8-inch CIPP, and contractors are encouraged to provide pricing for each option. Existing sewer pipe materials include vitrified clay pipe (VCP) and concrete pipe. Manholes may be traditional or drop manholes, in various conditions. Rehabilitating thirteen (13), 5-foot diameter, sanitary sewer manholes, where existing stairs are to be removed, interior walls cleaned and latent material removed, inverts raised and reshaped, walls built back with structural cementitious material, active infiltration stopped, and 137-vertical feet of topcoat lining installed. Two (2) manhole rims are to be raised 6-inches above grade and one (1) manhole rim is to be replaced. These are located in unimproved grassed areas. Work will require continuous sewer bypassing, to be completed by the Contractor. The Work will take place throughout the District’s service area, within the City of Centennial and Greenwood Village. All permitting, traffic control, cleaning, protection of the sewers, excavating, asphalt and concrete removal and replacement, CCTV, CIPP lining, sanitary service reactivation, bypass pumping, infiltration stopping, bench build back, cementitious build back, top coating, material and coating testing, surface restoration, site restoration, and abandonments, shall be performed by the Contractor as part of this Work. The Work is expected to be performed in live flow with continuous bypass pumping required. All sanitary sewer flows shall be verified by the Contractor prior to installing and operating the temporary bypass pumping at each location. Contract Documents, including Drawings and Specifications, may be obtained electronically by emailing For security reasons, you must enable JavaScript to view this E-mail address. beginning June 5, 2026. A Bid Bond or certified check payable to the Owner in the amount of 10% of the amount bid must be submitted with each Bid. A virtual pre-bid conference will be held at 1:00 p.m., June 18, 2026. Requests for meeting invite can be made to Erik Vik, PE ( For security reasons, you must enable JavaScript to view this E-mail address. ). Attendance is recommended but not required to submit bid documents. Bids shall be in accordance with the Bid Documents. The successful Bidder will be required to furnish payment and performance bonds required by law for public works projects and provide satisfactory evidence of insurance coverage required by the Contract. The Owner reserves the right to reject any and all Bids and to waive any informalities and irregularities in any Bids.

title: Log Cabin Disassembly and Removal
summary: Ouray County is requesting bids for the purchase and total removal of a log cabin kit home located at our Colona Colorado Gravel pit. The cabin has been unoccupied for over 20 years. The property is located in Montrose County; all applicable permits would need to be through Montrose County. The cabin may be viewed by appointment on Monday June 22 nd between 9:00 am and 3:00 pm. All bids must be placed in a sealed envelope labeled Cabin Removal and submitted to Ouray County Administration at the Ouray County Courthouse, 541 Fourth Street, Ouray CO 81427, by Wednesday July 8 th 2026 by 3:00 pm. Bid award will not be solely based on highest bidder, but on best proposal period to expedite and complete the demolition and related material removal. Ouray County reserves the right to reject any and all bids or to accept any bid that in its judgment best serves the County. The successful bidder shall be required to comply with all Local, State and Federal requirements related to the project. Scope of work: The complete and total disassembly and removal of log cabin shell and all related building components, contents and materials down to the foundation. The recycling of any building materials possible is highly encouraged. Successful bidder is responsible for all disposal fees, permits and related costs for hauling away cabin shell and all remaining building materials. A base time of demolition must be included with the bid that clearly states time needed to complete the removal.

title: MEADOW LANE CULVERT REPLACEMENT
summary: The project consists of removal and replacement of an existing culvert with two upsized culverts and associated riprap. The work also includes, but is not limited to, sidewalk and asphalt removal and replacement and landscaping. Currently, the culvert is undersized for the 100-year storm event, creating the potential for overtopping, piping around the culvert, and eventual washout of the roads. The culvert crosses beneath Meadow Lane, a collector street in southeast Steamboat Springs, and conveys the flows of a tributary of Walton Creek known as the Bear Creek tributary. The project area is located within the Walton Creek watershed, and is Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) regulated and labeled as FEMA Zone A. Construction is anticipated in late August or early September of 2026, and is expected to last approximately six to eight weeks. Proposals must be submitted via the electronic submission on BidNet ( https://www.bidnetdirect.com/colorado/cityofsteamboatsprings)

title: Harvest Grass Hay on County-Owned Property
summary: Request for Proposal Harvest Grass Hay on County-Owned Property The Board of County Commissioners intends to contract with a vendor/operator for a three (3) year period with annual renewal to harvest grass hay on county property known as the Deer Creek Meadow. It is estimated that approximately 15-30 tons of hay will be harvested. All bids will be based on a price per ton for all hay harvested and should reflect a fair market value at the time the hay is harvested. BID PREPARATION Interested parties should submit one (1) copy of their bid, no later than October 1, 2024, to the attention of Mike Smith, Interim County Manager. Submittals may be mailed to PO Box 1373, Fairplay, CO 80440, or emailed to For security reasons, you must enable JavaScript to view this E-mail address. . Submittals should contain a cover letter, not exceeding two pages, and concise, informative responses to each of the selection criteria listed below. Other information may be included, as well. EVALUATION CRITERIA The Commissioners will evaluate each bid based on the following factors: - General background of bidding party. - Experience with similar operation contracts. Include references. - What estimated time frame will all the hay be harvested and removed from the property. SELECTION PROCEDURE Based on the information contained in the bid, an interview committee will determine which party could best meet the needs of the County. The interview committee will initiate negotiations with the successful party and make a recommendation to the Board of County Commissioners for approval. The County reserves the right to reject any or all proposals received. Questions regarding this request for proposal (RFP) should be directed to Stevie Wilcox, For security reasons, you must enable JavaScript to view this E-mail address. , or 719-836-4245.
